info.title: PolyAI Conversations API v3
info.description:
Query conversations, recordings, and debug chat for a PolyAI agent.

Compared to v1, v3:
- Exposes additional turn-level fields (latency, translated_user_input, english_agent_response).
- Normalizes empty text fields as empty strings rather than null.

tags.name: Debug Chat
tags.description: Create and respond to debug chat sessions for testing an agent.

get.description: Retrieves conversations for a given account and project. Supports three retrieval modes: transcript search, semantic search, and random sampling. When no retrieval mode is specified, the endpoint returns all conversations matching the given filters (default behavior). Only one retrieval mode may be specified per request.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Account ID

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Project ID

parameters.name: query_type
parameters.description: Search mode. Use `transcript` for full-text search over conversation transcripts, or `semantic` for vector similarity search. Requires `query` to be set. Mutually exclusive with `sample`.

parameters.name: sample
parameters.description: Sampling mode. Use `random` to return a random sample of conversations within the time range. No `query` required. Mutually exclusive with `query_type`.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search text used for transcript or semantic search. Required when `query_type` is set.

parameters.name: turn_type
parameters.description: Transcript search only. Filter by `user` or `agent` turns. Defaults to both.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: One or more channels to filter by (e.g. voice, chat). Can be specified multiple times.

parameters.name: client_env
parameters.description: Client Environment - sandbox, pre-release or live

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: The start of the time range of the conversations to get, in ISO8601 format

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: The end of the time range of the conversations to get, in ISO8601 format

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of conversations to return per API call. If the result set of your query is larger than this, it will be paginated.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset within result set to fetch. Prefer `cursor` for paginating through large result sets — offset-based pagination becomes progressively slower at scale and may drift when conversations are written concurrently.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for keyset pagination. Omit on the first request, then pass the `cursor` value returned by the previous response to fetch the next page. Cursors are URL-safe, encode the position within the result set, and remain stable as new conversations are written. Mutually preferable to `offset` for paginating through large result sets. Returns a 400 if the cursor value is malformed.

parameters.name: variant_id
parameters.description: If it is set, we would return all conversations under this variant_id. Note that you may only specify either one of the variant_id or variant_name.

parameters.name: variant_name
parameters.description: If it is set, we would return all conversations under this variant_name. Note that you may only specify either one of the variant_id or variant_name. Also, make sure to encode any spaces in the name with "%20"

parameters.name: in_progress
parameters.description: If it is set to false, we only return finished conversations. If it is set to true, we only return conversations still in progress. By default, this parameter is None and we return all conversations.

parameters.name: include_latency
parameters.description: If true, include the latency info for each conversation.

get.description:
Retrieves a single conversation, including turn-by-turn transcript, metrics, and function events.

**Host:** this endpoint is served from `api.{region}.poly.ai` — *not* the `api.{region}-1.platform.polyai.app` host used by the list-conversations and audio endpoints.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Agent ID.

parameters.name: conversationId
parameters.description: Unique conversation ID.

get.summary: Get audio recording for a conversation
get.description:
Returns the audio recording for a conversation as a WAV file.

**Host:** this endpoint is served from `api.{region}.poly.ai` — *not* the `api.{region}-1.platform.polyai.app` host used by the list-conversations endpoint.

**PII access enforcement.** Users without PII access always receive the redacted recording, regardless of the `redacted` query parameter. Users with PII access receive the raw recording by default and can opt into the redacted version by passing `redacted=true`. PII access is granted through your account's role and permission configuration in Agent Studio.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Agent ID.

parameters.name: conversationId
parameters.description: Unique conversation ID.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Which side of the call to return. `combined` returns the full mixed recording. `user` returns only the caller audio. `agent` returns only the agent audio.

parameters.name: redacted
parameters.description: If `true`, returns the redacted recording with personally identifiable information (PII) muted. If `false`, returns the raw recording. **Users without PII access always receive the redacted recording, regardless of this parameter.**

200.description: WAV audio recording for the conversation.

404.description: Recording not found for the given conversation.

post.summary: Create a new debug chat session
post.description:
Starts a new debug chat session against an agent. Use this to interactively test an agent variant outside of production traffic.

**Host:** this endpoint is served from `api.{region}.poly.ai` — *not* the `api.{region}-1.platform.polyai.app` host used by the list-conversations and audio endpoints.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Agent ID.

post.summary: Send a message to a debug chat session
post.description:
Sends a user turn into an existing debug chat session and returns the agent's response.

**Host:** this endpoint is served from `api.{region}.poly.ai` — *not* the `api.{region}-1.platform.polyai.app` host used by the list-conversations and audio endpoints.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Agent ID.

parameters.name: conversationId
parameters.description: Debug chat session conversation ID returned by `POST /v1/agents/{agentId}/debug-chat`.

user_input.description: The transcription of what the user spoke or typed. May be an empty string when no input is present.

user_input_dtmf.description: DTMF digits captured for this turn (e.g. keypad input). May be an empty string when no DTMF was entered.

user_input_datetime.description: The time when the agent received the user's input. For speech, this is when ASR finished transcribing.

agent_response.description: The transcription of the agent's response to the user. May be an empty string when no response was produced.

agent_response_datetime.description: The time when the agent responded. For speech, this is when the agent started speaking.

latency.description: Agent latency for this turn (for example, time in seconds between receiving user input and starting the agent response).

translated_user_input.description: User input translated into the agent's working language (typically English) if translation is enabled. May be an empty string when not applicable.

english_agent_response.description: Agent response in English when translation is enabled. May be an empty string when not applicable.

intents.description: The list of intents that were found in this turn's input.

entities.description: The list of entities that were found in this turn's input.

is_ood.description: Whether the turn is classified as Out of Domain (OOD).

is_silence.description: Whether the turn is classified as a silence.

ConversationSummaryResponse.description: Public-facing conversation summary (used in list endpoint).

account_id.description: ID of the customer account this conversation is under.

project_id.description: ID of the project this conversation is under.

variant_id.description: ID of the variant this conversation is in.

variant_name.description: Name of the variant this conversation is in.

environment.description: Which deployment environment this conversation was handled in. Usually `live`, `sandbox`, or `pre-release`.

started_at.description: The time when the conversation began, in ISO8601 format.

channel.description: What medium the conversation took place in. Usually one of `WEBCHAT` or `VOICE-SIP`.

to_number.description: Phone number of the agent the caller was connected to.

in_progress.description: If true, the call is currently in progress, and therefore its properties may still change in the future.

num_turns.description: Total number of turns in the conversation. This count should match the length of the turns array.

total_duration.description: Duration of the entire call, in seconds.

polyai_duration.description: Duration of the portion of the call handled by the PolyAI agent, in seconds.

handoff.description: Whether or not the PolyAI agent handed the call off.

handoff_reason.description: Brief reason why the agent handed the call off.

handoff_destination.description: If there are multiple handoff destinations, which one was used.

num_silences.description: Number of turns in the conversation where the caller gave no discernible input.

num_ood.description: Number of turns in the conversation where the caller gave some input, but it could not be understood by our agent.

metrics.description: Mapping name -> value of custom metrics logged by the agent. If a metric name was written more than once, the value will be a list of the values that were written. Timestamps, where present, use a consistent ISO8601 date-time format.

state.description: Mapping of keys -> values written to the dialogue state by the agent.

turns.description: Ordered list of turn objects in the conversation. May be empty if transcript access is disabled in the project's API configuration, even when num_turns is non-zero.

ConversationDetailResponse.description: Public-facing conversation detail (includes turns, metrics, and function events).

accountId.description: ID of the customer account this conversation is under.

projectId.description: ID of the project this conversation is under.

variantId.description: Variant ID the conversation ran in.

inProgress.description: Whether the conversation is still in progress.

handoffDestination.description: If multiple handoff destinations exist, which one was used.

note.description: Free-text note saved on the conversation.

metrics.description: Custom metrics recorded for the conversation.

functionEvents.description: Function execution events that occurred during the conversation.

GetConversationsResponse.description: A page of conversations matching the query, plus pagination data.

next_offset.description: If present, the result is being paginated and requires another API call to fetch the next portion (pass the provided `next_offset` as the `offset` param for the next API call). For new integrations, prefer the `cursor` field for faster, drift-free pagination.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor pointing to the start of the next page. Pass this value as the `cursor` query parameter on the next request to fetch the following page. `null` when there are no more pages.

ChatMetadataResponse.description: Metadata returned with each chat response.

agentResponseAudioUrl.description: URL to the synthesized audio for the agent response, when applicable.

CreateDebugChatBody.description: Starting parameters for a debug chat session: which agent variant, which language codes, and which environment to run in.

asrLangCode.description: ASR language code (e.g. `en-US`). Defaults to server config.

ttsLangCode.description: TTS language code (e.g. `en-US`). Defaults to server config.

conversationId.description: Custom conversation ID. Auto-generated if omitted.

integrationAttributes.description: Custom attributes accessible via `conv.integration_attributes` in project functions.

RespondDebugChatBody.description: A user turn sent into an existing debug chat session, with the runtime overrides that apply to this message.

asrLangCode.description: ASR language code (e.g. `en-US`). Defaults to server config.

ttsLangCode.description: TTS language code (e.g. `en-US`). Defaults to server config.

DebugChatResponse.description: A chat turn's result: the agent's reply text, the conversation's updated state, and whether the conversation has ended.

conversationId.description: ID of the debug chat session.

userInput.description: User input that produced this response.

conversationEnded.description: Whether the conversation has ended after this turn.

delayedResponse.description: Whether the agent's response was delayed (e.g. awaiting a long-running tool).
